task_completion.description: Open the textbook price data in the spreadsheet application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: LibreOffice Calc is visible with the textbook data loaded, showing rows for 8 books (e.g., CHEM 101, BIO 200).

task_completion.description: Clean and format the data to ensure accuracy and readability.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet shows formatting improvements, such as currency symbols applied to price columns, bolded headers, or adjusted column widths.

task_completion.description: Calculate the total cost for each item, including shipping fees and access codes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Additional columns or cells display the calculated total costs for the different purchasing options (Campus Bookstore, Amazon, Student Marketplace). The totals should incorporate the $85 access code and $5 shipping fee where applicable.

task_completion.description: Identify the best deals for each textbook.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A specific column or set of cells explicitly identifies the lowest price (best deal) among the available options for each of the 8 textbooks.

task_completion.description: Calculate the total cost of the best deals and evaluate it against the budget.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A cell shows the sum of all best deals (expected to be around $1272) and there is evidence of comparing this total to the $600 budget limit.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreOffice Calc GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show LibreOffice Calc open and being interacted with, rather than terminal commands or Python scripts modifying the file directly.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the spreadsheet to input formulas and format data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active cell selections, the formula bar being used, or formatting menus/dialogs open.

integrity.description: The agent used formulas for calculations rather than pasting pre-calculated results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The formula bar shows actual formulas (e.g., MIN, IF, SUM) being used for calculations rather than raw numbers being typed directly into the result cells.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 8 textbooks. To calculate total costs, the agent must add a $5 default shipping cost (if shipping isn't free) and an $85 access code cost (if required). The budget limit is $600. The sum of the best deals will actually total around $1272, which exceeds the budget. The agent should calculate this sum (acceptable range $400-$2000) and identify the best deal for each book. The verifier checks for the use of IF formulas and MIN formulas.
